perturbation.original_text: c. Integrity's responsibilities. Integrity will obtain master lease and print agreements with all third party owners of masters embodied in the Product at its own expense. Mechanical rates will not exceed [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the then-current statutory rate, and Print licenses will not exceed a prorated portion of [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price, Integrity will also manufacture the Product and sell to TL its requirements for resale.
perturbation.changed_text: c. Integrity's responsibilities. Integrity will obtain master lease and print agreements with all third party owners of masters embodied in the Product at its own expense. Integrity will also manufacture the Product and sell to TL its requirements for resale.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific limitations on mechanical rates and print licenses, the contract becomes vague regarding the financial terms Integrity must adhere to when securing these licenses. This omission creates uncertainty and could lead to disputes over what constitutes a reasonable expense. The specific rates are mentioned later in the document, which generates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. Integrity will, upon receipt of approved purchase orders from TL, sell to TL in non-returnable box lot quantities, TL's requirements products rates calculated on the following basis (plus freight). The copyright royalty on recorded products is based on [**]/1/% of the then current statutory mechanical rate, and on song books, [**]/1/ percent ([**]/1/%) of the retail selling price (prorated to each copyright holder). Integrity may adjust the prices charged to TL on each volume to accurately reflect the then-current royalty obligation to all parties, including Integrity.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified maximum rates for mechanical and print licenses. The changed text removes these specific limitations. The contradicted text refers to rates calculated based on a percentage of the statutory mechanical rate and retail selling price. By removing the limitations in the changed text, Integrity could potentially charge rates higher than initially implied, creating a contradiction with the intention behind calculating rates based on a fixed percentage as outlined in the contradicted text. This creates uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding the financial terms Integrity is bound by when securing licenses. The omission directly undermines the basis upon which royalty rates are calculated, as specified in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: d. Mailing Lists. The parties hereby agree that they will mutually share mailing list data of purchasers of the products created hereunder in exchange for names of equal value, (i.e., an expired name for an expired name, active buyer for active buyer.) TL specifically agrees to supply Integrity with data on former subscribers to its "Songs 4 Life" series, who have subsequently canceled for the express purpose of soliciting Product continuity subscriptions.
perturbation.changed_text: d. Mailing Lists. The parties hereby agree that they may share mailing list data. TL specifically agrees to supply Integrity with data on former subscribers to its "Songs 4 Life" series, who have subsequently canceled for the express purpose of soliciting Product continuity subscriptions.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will mutually share' to 'may share', the obligation to share mailing lists becomes discretionary. This contradicts the implicit understanding of equal benefit established in the original text, because the text continues TL's obligation to supply data. Thus, if Integrity chooses not to share its list, TL is providing it but not getting the same reciprocal value.
perturbation.contradicted_text: d. Mailing Lists. The parties hereby agree that they may share mailing list data. TL specifically agrees to supply Integrity with data on former subscribers to its "Songs 4 Life" series, who have subsequently canceled for the express purpose of soliciting Product continuity subscriptions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'will mutually share' to 'may share', making the sharing of mailing lists discretionary. This creates a contradiction because TL is still obligated to provide data while Integrity is not obligated to reciprocate. The original agreement intended for equal value exchange which is now broken by the modification. The change directly conflicts with the implicit assumption of mutual benefit and reciprocity, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: b. TL Trademarks. TL hereby grants to Integrity the right to use its "TL Music" name and logo ("the TL Trademarks") in connection with the products produced during the Term of this Agreement for as long as the parties continue to sell and distribute such products at no additional cost to Integrity, and in accordance with the terms and conditions contained herein. Integrity will honor the notice requirement relating to the Integrity Trademarks and will place such trademarks on all products and advertising produced hereunder in accordance with the applicable regulations and TL guidelines. TL warrants that it has all rights to grant Integrity the right to use the TL Trademarks and will indemnify and hold Integrity harmless with respect thereto. Integrity recognizes TL's title to the TL Trademarks and will not at any time do or suffer to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair TL's rights in and to the TL Trademarks. It is understood that Integrity will not acquire and will not claim any title to the TL Trademarks adverse to TL by virtue of this license, or through Integrity's use of the TL Trademarks.
perturbation.changed_text: b. TL Trademarks. TL hereby grants to Integrity the right to use its "TL Music" name and logo ("the TL Trademarks") in connection with the products produced during the Term of this Agreement. TL warrants that it has all rights to grant Integrity the right to use the TL Trademarks and will indemnify and hold Integrity harmless with respect thereto. Integrity recognizes TL's title to the TL Trademarks and will not at any time do or suffer to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair TL's rights in and to the TL Trademarks. It is understood that Integrity will not acquire and will not claim any title to the TL Trademarks adverse to TL by virtue of this license, or through Integrity's use of the TL Trademarks.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Integrity will honor the notice requirement relating to the Integrity Trademarks and will place such trademarks on all products and advertising produced hereunder in accordance with the applicable regulations and TL guidelines,' a contradiction is introduced. Previously, Integrity was explicitly required to follow TL's guidelines regarding trademark usage. Without this clause, while Integrity still has the right to use the TL Trademarks, the specific manner and conditions of their usage are undefined, creating an inconsistency regarding the obligations of Integrity in trademark usage.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b. TL Trademarks. TL hereby grants to Integrity the right to use its "TL Music" name and logo ("the TL Trademarks") in connection with the products produced during the Term of this Agreement. TL warrants that it has all rights to grant Integrity the right to use the TL Trademarks and will indemnify and hold Integrity harmless with respect thereto. Integrity recognizes TL's title to the TL Trademarks and will not at any time do or suffer to be done any act or thing which will in any way impair TL's rights in and to the TL Trademarks. It is understood that Integrity will not acquire and will not claim any title to the TL Trademarks adverse to TL by virtue of this license, or through Integrity's use of the TL Trademarks.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that Integrity 'will honor the notice requirement relating to the Integrity Trademarks and will place such trademarks on all products and advertising produced hereunder in accordance with the applicable regulations and TL guidelines.' This clause imposed a clear obligation on Integrity regarding trademark usage. The changed text removes this obligation, creating a contradiction. While Integrity still has the right to use the TL Trademarks as stated in the Contradicted Text, the specific manner and conditions of their usage are now undefined, directly conflicting with the original intention of enforcing TL trademark guidelines.
